title: Entadopsis hockii
intro: Entadopsis hockii, also known as Hock's entada or Hock's bean, is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, characterized by its unique bean-like pods and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may help in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Entadopsis hockii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and dried extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Entadopsis hockii belongs to the family Fabaceae. It is a genus within the subfamily Faboideae. The exact classification of Entadopsis hockii may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be a flowering plant.
